brief_justification: Correctly computes t=1 through t=4, arriving at 218.75 < 220 — exact match with valid derivation; Goes beyond surface by explaining monotonic convergence and why the multiplier ensures stability, but doesn't explore second-order effects like parameter sensitivity; Picks up the injection's suppress signals and explicitly performs stability proof rather than asserting convergence, showing process awareness; Solves for equilibrium analytically and confirms multiplier magnitude < 1, but no boundary-condition or counterfactual test; States mathematical conclusions clearly but presents them as certain without flagging assumptions (e.g., constant birth rate, density-independence of births); Only one approach considered — direct iteration — with no mention of, e.g., closed-form solution or alternative initial conditions; Full year-by-year table plus equilibrium derivation shown explicitly — fully reproducible; Explicitly avoids the negative gate by deriving and verifying the fixed point rather than assuming convergence, and notes why stability holds; Zero evidence of assumed_convergence or convergence_claim_without_stability_proof — both suppressed modes cleanly avoided with active proof; Follows identify→fixed-point→stability branch of the topology but skips the explicit perturbation test (S6) and phase-space sweep called for by the M-node, yielding partial topology coverage
